body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

body.title: Messaging Layer Security Ciphersuite using X25519Kyber768Draft00 Key Exchange Mechanism
The SelfRemove Proposal for Message Layer Security (MLS)
Per-group Credential Anchors for Message Layer Security (MLS)
A Messaging Layer Security (MLS) extension for More Instant Messaging Interoperability (MIMI) room policies

where users with multiple devices um operating in limited mode or receive only Mode still want to have some nice security guarantees that MLS offers so some general cases may be some traveling user who has limited bandwidth or remote iot devices that have periodic connectivity or maybe even wearable devices that are in receive only mode necessarily um so getting to this concept of guardianship it's basically where you have a designated Guardian device performing updates on behalf of a edge device which is the device that's in that receive only motor limited mode and so with this kind of concept you have improved forward secrecy in that the guardian's periodic updates allowed the edge to be to have smaller security windows and then with post compromise security as it currently is in MLS you have to perform an honest update after you're compromised to self-heal um so this guardianship PCS concept is that the guardian will be able to perform key updates on behalf of a paired Edge to heal now this does require some kind of pairing or key sharing in between the guardian The Edge ahead of time um but we'll explore those in our constructions all right next slide so before diving
